Orjan Nyland.bio: Experienced Norwegian goalkeeper who plays for Sevilla in La Liga. With 45 caps, he has been a reliable presence between the posts and brings top-level European experience to the squad.
Orjan Nyland.playingStyle: Athletic goalkeeper with sharp reflexes, good distribution, and experience in high-pressure La Liga matches
Orjan Nyland.worldCup2026Role: Starting goalkeeper bringing La Liga pedigree and tournament reliability
Orjan Nyland.notableFact: One of few Norwegian goalkeepers to have played regularly in La Liga, one of the world's most demanding leagues

Marcus Pedersen.bio: Young right-back playing for Feyenoord in the Eredivisie. A modern full-back who combines defensive ability with attacking output, he has 20 caps for Norway.
Marcus Pedersen.playingStyle: Athletic, attack-minded right-back who provides width and overlapping runs while maintaining defensive discipline
Marcus Pedersen.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back or strong backup behind Julian Ryerson, providing attacking thrust from the flank
Marcus Pedersen.notableFact: Has gained valuable European experience through Feyenoord's Champions League campaigns

Kristoffer Ajer.bio: Tall, commanding center-back who plays for Brentford in the Premier League. A former Celtic standout, his combination of size, pace, and ball-playing ability make him one of Norway's most important defenders.
Kristoffer Ajer.playingStyle: Ball-playing center-back who uses his height and long stride to carry the ball out of defense and launch attacks
Kristoffer Ajer.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back and key figure in Norway's build-up play from defense
Kristoffer Ajer.notableFact: Stands 198cm tall, making him one of the tallest outfield players at the 2026 World Cup

Stefan Strandberg.bio: Veteran center-back with Serie A experience at Salernitana. With 45 caps, he brings a wealth of international experience and Italian defensive education to Norway's backline.
Stefan Strandberg.playingStyle: Traditional, no-nonsense center-back who prioritizes positioning, marking, and winning aerial duels
Stefan Strandberg.worldCup2026Role: Experienced backup center-back who provides reliability and calm in high-pressure situations
Stefan Strandberg.notableFact: Has played in multiple countries including Italy, gaining a diverse tactical education throughout his career

Birger Meling.bio: Left-back playing for Rennes in Ligue 1, bringing French football experience to Norway's squad. A natural left-footer with 45 caps, he provides balance and quality from the left side of defense.
Birger Meling.playingStyle: Technical left-back with excellent crossing ability, good positional sense, and comfort on the ball
Birger Meling.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back or strong competitor for the position, providing quality delivery from wide positions
Birger Meling.notableFact: Plays alongside Jordan's Mousa Al-Taamari at Rennes, connecting two World Cup 2026 nations at club level

Morten Thorsby.bio: Physical defensive midfielder from Union Berlin in the Bundesliga. With 50 caps, he is an established member of Norway's midfield and known for his tireless running and environmental activism.
Morten Thorsby.playingStyle: Aggressive, high-energy midfielder who presses relentlessly and wins the ball back in dangerous areas
Morten Thorsby.worldCup2026Role: Starting defensive midfielder providing the engine and defensive shield for Norway's talented attackers
Morten Thorsby.notableFact: Well-known environmental activist who has used his football platform to advocate for sustainability and climate action

Sander Berge.bio: Imposing central midfielder at Fulham in the Premier League. At 195cm, he combines remarkable technical ability with physical dominance, making him one of the most complete midfielders in Scandinavian football.
Sander Berge.playingStyle: Towering yet technically gifted midfielder who controls the tempo, makes progressive passes, and dominates physical battles
Sander Berge.worldCup2026Role: Key central midfielder alongside Odegaard, providing the physical and technical backbone of Norway's midfield
Sander Berge.notableFact: Was signed by Sheffield United for a then-club record fee before moving to Fulham, reflecting his high standing in European football

Erling Haaland.bio: Manchester City's superstar striker and one of the most prolific goalscorers in world football. With 31 goals in just 35 caps for Norway, he is the undisputed focal point of the entire national team.
Erling Haaland.playingStyle: Devastating striker combining extraordinary pace, lethal finishing, and immense physical power to overwhelm defenses
Erling Haaland.worldCup2026Role: Norway's talisman and primary goal threat -- the entire team is built around supplying him with chances
Erling Haaland.notableFact: Has shattered Premier League and Champions League scoring records at Manchester City, and the 2026 World Cup will be his first major international tournament

Martin Odegaard.bio: Arsenal's captain and Norway's creative genius, widely regarded as one of the best playmakers in world football. With 60 caps and 9 goals, he orchestrates everything Norway does in attack.
Martin Odegaard.playingStyle: Supremely technical playmaker with extraordinary vision, creative passing, and the ability to unlock any defense
Martin Odegaard.worldCup2026Role: Captain, chief creator, and the player responsible for connecting midfield to Haaland -- Norway's most important creative player
Martin Odegaard.notableFact: Became the youngest player to debut for Real Madrid at just 16, and has since developed into one of the Premier League's finest players at Arsenal

Mohamed Elyounoussi.bio: Experienced left winger with 60 caps and 11 goals who plays for Celtic in Scotland. A versatile attacker of Moroccan descent, he brings international experience and reliability to Norway's flanks.
Mohamed Elyounoussi.playingStyle: Intelligent winger who combines well with teammates, makes clever runs, and contributes goals and assists consistently
Mohamed Elyounoussi.worldCup2026Role: Experienced wide option providing reliable attacking output and tactical flexibility
Mohamed Elyounoussi.notableFact: Eligible for both Norway and Morocco, he chose to represent Norway and has become one of their most capped wingers

Sten Grytebust.bio: Backup goalkeeper playing for Copenhagen in Denmark's top flight. With 10 caps, he provides experienced depth behind Nyland in the goalkeeping department.
Sten Grytebust.playingStyle: Solid, reliable goalkeeper with good handling and the ability to organize his defense effectively
Sten Grytebust.worldCup2026Role: Second-choice goalkeeper providing dependable backup and training competition
Sten Grytebust.notableFact: Has gained valuable Champions League experience through Copenhagen's European campaigns

Julian Ryerson.bio: Borussia Dortmund right-back who has established himself as one of the Bundesliga's most consistent full-backs. His 20 caps for Norway reflect his rapid rise at both club and international level.
Julian Ryerson.playingStyle: Energetic, two-way full-back who excels at both attacking overlaps and defensive recovery
Julian Ryerson.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back bringing Champions League experience and high-level tactical awareness
Julian Ryerson.notableFact: Was part of Borussia Dortmund's run to the Champions League final, gaining invaluable big-match experience

Fredrik Midtsjo.bio: Experienced central midfielder from AZ Alkmaar in the Eredivisie. With 35 caps, he brings tactical intelligence and consistency to Norway's midfield options.
Fredrik Midtsjo.playingStyle: Disciplined, hard-working midfielder who excels at screening the defense and keeping possession moving
Fredrik Midtsjo.worldCup2026Role: Midfield rotation option providing balance and defensive stability when needed
Fredrik Midtsjo.notableFact: Was a key figure in AZ Alkmaar's strong Eredivisie and Conference League campaigns

Leo Ostigard.bio: Young center-back at Napoli in Serie A, one of the most prestigious defensive schools in football. His 20 caps and experience in Italian football make him a valuable defensive asset.
Leo Ostigard.playingStyle: Aggressive, front-foot defender who is strong in the air and combative in ground duels
Leo Ostigard.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back candidate whose Serie A experience has sharpened his defensive skills
Leo Ostigard.notableFact: Was part of Napoli's historic Serie A title-winning squad, the first in over 30 years

Patrick Berg.bio: Creative midfielder from Bodo/Glimt who was instrumental in the club's rise to prominence in Norwegian and European football. His 15 caps reflect his growing international stature.
Patrick Berg.playingStyle: Technical midfielder with excellent passing range, creative vision, and the ability to control the tempo of matches
Patrick Berg.worldCup2026Role: Midfield depth option who can step in and provide creative quality when called upon
Patrick Berg.notableFact: Son of former Norwegian international Orn Berg, continuing a family tradition in Norwegian football

Fredrik Aursnes.bio: Highly rated midfielder at Benfica in the Portuguese league, one of Europe's elite clubs. His 25 caps for Norway reflect his consistent quality at the highest level.
Fredrik Aursnes.playingStyle: Versatile, intelligent midfielder who reads the game superbly and contributes in all phases of play
Fredrik Aursnes.worldCup2026Role: Key midfield player whose versatility and quality make him a likely starter in Norway's engine room
Fredrik Aursnes.notableFact: His move from Norwegian football to Benfica via Feyenoord shows a steady progression to Europe's elite level

Kristian Thorstvedt.bio: Energetic midfielder at Sassuolo in Serie A, son of former Tottenham Hotspur goalkeeper Erik Thorstvedt. With 31 caps and 4 goals, he adds dynamism to Norway's midfield.
Kristian Thorstvedt.playingStyle: Dynamic, goal-scoring midfielder who makes late runs into the box and combines physical power with technical quality
Kristian Thorstvedt.worldCup2026Role: Midfield option who adds a goal threat from central areas and energy in both directions
Kristian Thorstvedt.notableFact: Son of former Tottenham and Norway goalkeeper Erik Thorstvedt, following his father's footsteps in Norwegian football

Antonio Nusa.bio: Teenage sensation at RB Leipzig in the Bundesliga, widely regarded as one of the most exciting young talents in European football. At just 21, his pace and skill are electrifying.
Antonio Nusa.playingStyle: Explosive young winger with dazzling dribbling, blistering pace, and the fearlessness to take on any defender
Antonio Nusa.worldCup2026Role: Exciting attacking wildcard whose skill and unpredictability can change any match in an instant
Antonio Nusa.notableFact: Became the youngest Norwegian to score in the Champions League when he netted for Club Brugge before his move to Leipzig

Alexander Sorloth.bio: Powerful striker at Atletico Madrid in La Liga, providing Norway with a lethal alternative to Haaland. With 14 goals in 50 caps, he is a proven international goalscorer in his own right.
Alexander Sorloth.playingStyle: Tall, physical striker who excels at hold-up play, aerial duels, and finishing inside the box
Alexander Sorloth.worldCup2026Role: Second striker option who can partner Haaland or provide a different attacking dimension off the bench
Alexander Sorloth.notableFact: Scored at a remarkable rate during his time in the Turkish league before earning moves to Europe's elite clubs

Jorgen Strand Larsen.bio: Emerging striker at Crystal Palace in the Premier League who has become a key part of Norway's forward line. With 24 caps and 4 goals, the tall forward adds quality depth behind Haaland.
Jorgen Strand Larsen.playingStyle: Tall, mobile striker who combines aerial prowess with good link-up play and intelligent positioning
Jorgen Strand Larsen.worldCup2026Role: Forward rotation option who can start alongside or instead of Haaland depending on tactical needs
Jorgen Strand Larsen.notableFact: His move to Crystal Palace from Celta Vigo capped a rapid rise through European football

Jens Petter Hauge.bio: Former AC Milan winger now back at Bodo/Glimt who made headlines with his performances in the Europa League. With 15 caps and 1 goal, he brings European experience and attacking flair.
Jens Petter Hauge.playingStyle: Skillful left winger with pace, good dribbling technique, and the ability to score and create goals
Jens Petter Hauge.worldCup2026Role: Wide attacking option providing creativity and goal threat from the left side
Jens Petter Hauge.notableFact: Scored a memorable goal against AC Milan in the Europa League for Bodo/Glimt before earning a move to the Italian giants

Marius Lode.bio: Veteran center-back from Bodo/Glimt who has been part of the club's remarkable journey from obscurity to European prominence. With 5 caps, he provides experienced defensive depth.
Marius Lode.playingStyle: Steady, reliable center-back who reads the game well and provides calm defensive cover
Marius Lode.worldCup2026Role: Experienced backup center-back providing squad depth and defensive reliability
Marius Lode.notableFact: Was part of Bodo/Glimt's incredible rise from the Norwegian second tier to European competition success

Oscar Bobb.bio: Exciting young talent at Manchester City who trains alongside some of the best players in the world. At 22, his raw potential and skillset make him one of Norway's most promising prospects.
Oscar Bobb.playingStyle: Tricky, creative winger with quick feet, excellent close control, and the ability to beat defenders in tight spaces
Oscar Bobb.worldCup2026Role: Young attacking prospect who can provide a spark off the bench with his skill and energy
Oscar Bobb.notableFact: Has impressed Pep Guardiola enough to earn regular first-team opportunities at Manchester City

Andreas Hanche-Olsen.bio: Center-back playing for Genoa in Serie A, gaining valuable experience in one of the world's most tactically demanding leagues. His 15 caps reflect his steady development.
Andreas Hanche-Olsen.playingStyle: Composed, technically sound defender who has absorbed the Italian defensive tradition and excels in tactical positioning
Andreas Hanche-Olsen.worldCup2026Role: Defensive squad depth option who brings Serie A tactical awareness to the group
Andreas Hanche-Olsen.notableFact: One of several Norwegian defenders who have moved to Italy, continuing a tradition of Scandinavians thriving in Serie A

Stale Solbakken.bio: Young midfielder at Roma in Serie A, representing the future of Norwegian football. At just 22 with 3 caps, his selection shows Norway's commitment to developing young talent at the highest level.
Stale Solbakken.playingStyle: Composed, intelligent young midfielder with good passing ability and an understanding of space and time
Stale Solbakken.worldCup2026Role: Young developmental talent gaining World Cup experience for the future of Norwegian football
Stale Solbakken.notableFact: Son of former Norway manager Stale Solbakken Sr., he is following in his father's football footsteps at one of Italy's most historic clubs
